title: QueueLog
description: A mod that lets you monitor and display block updates.
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/AE9z24odX5Y" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Description
This mod lets you monitor and display every block update that occurs in your world. I recommend using it in a superflat world, as tracking all updates in a normal world can quickly become overwhelming.
## Command operator /ql

<details> <summary>status</summary>
  Displays which options are currently enabled.
</details>
<details> <summary>tgldisplay</summary>
  Turns the visual display of block updates on or off.
</details>
<details> <summary>tglmonitoring</summary>
  Enables or disables monitoring of block updates. All monitored updates are recorded in the log.
</details>
<details> <summary>tglhighlight</summary>
  Turns the highlighting of the selected ghost block on or off.
</details>
<details> <summary>show</summary>
  Displays all logged block updates in chat, organized by category. </details> 
<details> <summary>show {x, y, z}</summary>
  Displays the number of block updates that occurred at a specific coordinate in chat.
</details>
<details> <summary>clear</summary> 
  Clears the block update log.
</details>

## Heads up
If you’re using shaders, some might hide or not render the block update visuals.

[Youtube Channel - ziprano](https://www.youtube.com/@ziprano)
